dashboard_recap.headline: MLB Daily Recap - May 17, 2026
dashboard_recap.article:
**MLB Recap: May 17, 2024**

The Tampa Bay Rays cruised to a 6-3 win over the Miami Marlins, with Drew Rasmussen earning the victory. Junior Caminero led the Marlins' offense with a home run and two RBI. Xavier Edwards and Otto Lopez also contributed with hits for Miami.

In a dominating performance, the Philadelphia Phillies blanked the Pittsburgh Pirates 6-0. Zack Wheeler threw seven innings of shutout ball, striking out eight and allowing just one walk. Bryson Stott clubbed a home run and drove in three runs to pace the Phillies' offense.

The Atlanta Braves routed the Boston Red Sox 8-1, with Austin Riley hitting a home run and driving in three runs. Grant Holmes earned the win, pitching six scoreless innings and striking out four.

The Cleveland Guardians pounded out ten runs on their way to a 10-3 win over the Cincinnati Reds. Kyle Manzardo hit two home runs and drove in three runs to lead the Guardians' offense. Gavin Williams earned the win, pitching six strong innings and striking out seven.

The Toronto Blue Jays edged the Detroit Tigers 4-1, with Vladimir Guerrero Jr. hitting a home run and driving in a run. Kevin Gausman earned the win, pitching six scoreless innings and striking out five.

The New York Mets came from behind to defeat the New York Yankees 7-6. Tyrone Taylor hit a home run and drove in three runs to lead the Mets' offense.

The Texas Rangers blanked the Houston Astros 8-0, with Nathan Eovaldi throwing seven shutout innings and striking out eight. Jake Burger hit a home run and drove in four runs to pace the Rangers' offense.

The Chicago White Sox outlasted the Chicago Cubs 9-8, with Edgar Quero hitting a home run and driving in three runs. Jacob Webb earned the win, pitching 1.1 scoreless innings and striking out three.

The Minnesota Twins edged the Milwaukee Brewers 5-4, with Ryan Jeffers hitting a home run and driving in a run. Bailey Ober earned the win, pitching 1.1 scoreless innings and striking out two.

The Kansas City Royals shut out the St. Louis Cardinals 2-0, with Salvador Perez hitting a home run and driving in two runs. Andre Pallante earned the win, pitching 6.2 scoreless innings and striking out seven.

The Arizona Diamondbacks edged the Colorado Rockies 8-6, with Corbin Carroll hitting two home runs and driving in three runs. Michael Soroka earned the win, pitching 5.2 strong innings and striking out eight.

The San Francisco Giants routed the Athletics 10-1, with Harrison Bader hitting a grand slam and driving in four runs. Adrian Houser earned the win, pitching 5.0 scoreless innings and striking out four.

The Los Angeles Dodgers dominated the Los Angeles Angels 10-1, with Roki Sasaki throwing seven strong innings and striking out eight. Kyle Tucker led the Dodgers' offense with three hits and three RBI.

The San Diego Padres edged the Seattle Mariners 8-3, with Gavin Sheets hitting two home runs and driving in four runs. Lucas Giolito earned the win, pitching 6.0 strong innings and striking out six.
